zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Verdun於1315年3月21日出生在英國英格蘭維爾爾阿姆斯波利時，她的父親Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun36歲，母親Elizabeth de Clare20歲。她於1331年2月20日之前與Henry de Ferrers II在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1349年7月25日在英國萊希斯特斯爾格洛比享年34歲，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾奧爾維爾史洛福特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Verdun于1315年3月21日出生在英国英格兰維爾爾阿姆斯波利时，她的父亲Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun36岁，母亲Elizabeth de Clare20岁。她于1331年2月20日之前与Henry de Ferrers II在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1349年7月25日在英国萊希斯特斯爾格洛比享年34岁，埋葬在英国萊希斯特斯爾奧爾維爾史洛福特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella

The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Verdun was born on 21 March 1315, in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, was 36 and her mother, Elizabeth de Clare, was 20. She married Henry de Ferrers II before 20 February 1331, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 25 July 1349, in Groby, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 34, and was buried in Ulverscroft, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Verdun est née le 21 mars 1315, à Amesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Clare, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Henry de Ferrers II avant 20 février 1331, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 juillet 1349, à Groby, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Ulverscroft, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Verdun am 21. März 1315 in Amesbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun 36 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Clare 20. Sie hat vor 20. Februar 1331 in England, United Kingdom Henry de Ferrers II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 25. Juli 1349 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Groby, Leicestershire, England und wurde in Ulverscroft, Leicestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Verdun è nata il il 21 marzo 1315 nel Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de Clare, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Henry de Ferrers II prima del 20 febbraio 1331, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 25 luglio 1349, nel Groby, Leicestershire, England, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolta a Ulverscroft, Leicestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Verdunが1315年3月21日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアエイメスブリーで生まれた時，父Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdunは36歳，母Elizabeth de Clareは20歳であった。1331年2月20日以前にイギリスイギリスでHenry de Ferrers IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1349年7月25日にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアグロビーで34歳で死去し，イギリスレアイセステルシュアイアウルバースクロフトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Verdun(이)가 1315년 3월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 아메스버이에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Clare(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1331년 2월 20일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Henry de Ferrers II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 7월 25일 영국 레이스스터실르 그로비에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 울버스크로프트에 묻혔다.

pt.21 March 1315–25 July 1349: 21 de março de 1315–25 de julho de 1349
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Verdun nasceu em 21 de março de 1315, em Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Clare, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry de Ferrers II antes 20 de fevereiro de 1331, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 25 de julho de 1349, em Groby, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em Ulverscroft, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Verdun родилась 21 марта 1315 г. в местности амесбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Clare, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry de Ferrers II перед 20 февраль 1331 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 25 июля 1349 г. в местности гроби, лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности улвирскрофт, лисестиршир, Англия.

es.21 March 1315–25 July 1349: 21 de marzo de 1315–25 de julio de 1349
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Verdun nació el 21 de marzo de 1315, en Amesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Clare, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Henry de Ferrers II antes de 20 de febrero de 1331, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 25 de julio de 1349, en Groby, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultada en Ulverscroft, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
